title: Carum nivale
taxonomy: Carum nivale is classified in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family includes various plants with hollow stems and umbrella-like flowers. Carum nivale is a member of the genus Carum, which comprises several species of plants, mainly native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The species within this genus are often characterized by their fern-like foliage and small white flowers. Carum nivale is often referred to as a rare or endangered species due to its limited distribution and habitat.

intro: Carum nivale, also known as Himalayan caraway or snow caraway, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Carum nivale include volatile oils, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Carum nivale is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for local application.
